title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Middle Eastern cuisine with our authentic Lamb Ouzi recipe. Learn to slow roast a whole lamb to tender perfection, served with aromatic rice. Get the full recipe!

seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Delight
h2Headings: What is Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted?

seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented, whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi recipe, garnished with nuts and fresh herbs, ready for a Middle Eastern feast.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of this traditional Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, a show-stopping dish perfect for special occasions.

description: Experience the rich flavors of a classic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, a festive dish combining tender, spiced lamb with aromatic rice and toasted nuts. This recipe offers a delightful, quicker approach to achieving the traditional taste of Ouzi, perfect for a family dinner or special gathering. Enjoy a truly satisfying and flavorful meal that will transport your taste buds to the heart of the Middle East.

ingredients.item: Boneless lamb shoulder or leg, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: or a small whole lamb roast if cooking time is extended
rinsed and soaked for 30 minutes, then drained
cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cardamom, black pepper, allspice

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Season the lamb cubes generously with salt, pepper, and 1 tablespoon of the Ouzi spice blend. Brown the lamb in batches until well-sealed on all sides, then set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil or ghee to the same pot. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer Lamb
instructions.text: Return the browned lamb to the pot with the onions and garlic. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of Ouzi spice blend and stir well to coat the lamb. Pour in 2 cups of the chicken or lamb broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until the lamb is tender.

instructions.title: Cook the Rice
instructions.text: While the lamb simmers, in a separate pot, bring the remaining 2 cups of broth to a boil. Add the drained basmati rice, salt to taste, and reduce heat to low. Cover and cook for 15 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.

instructions.title: Combine Ouzi Elements
instructions.text: Once the rice is cooked, gently fluff it with a fork. Stir the frozen peas and carrots into the lamb mixture and cook for 2-3 minutes until heated through. Combine the tender lamb and vegetable mixture with the cooked rice in a large serving platter.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the combined lamb and rice Ouzi to a large serving dish. Garnish generously with toasted slivered almonds, toasted pine nuts, and fresh chopped parsley or mint. Serve immediately as a complete and festive Middle Eastern meal.

tips: For deeper flavor, marinate the lamb cubes overnight with the Ouzi spice blend, a touch of olive oil, and lemon juice before browning.
If you cant find a pre-made Ouzi spice blend, create your own by mixing ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, allspice, black pepper, and a pinch of cardamom.
For an impressive presentation, press the finished Ouzi mixture into a large bundt pan or mold, then invert onto a serving platter before garnishing.
article: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a beloved Middle Eastern Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
